mainEntity.name: How much does a Range Rover Evoque P300 engine replacement cost in the UK?

acceptedAnswer.text: A reconditioned Evoque P300 (PT204) engine typically costs £3,500–£5,500 supply only. Fitting adds £2,000–£3,000 depending on location and workshop rates. Used engines start from £1,500–£3,000 supply only. Main dealer new engines start from £8,000–£12,000+ supply only.

mainEntity.name: Is the Range Rover Evoque P300 engine reliable?

acceptedAnswer.text: The PT204 Ingenium petrol has a mixed reputation. It's generally more reliable than the diesel Ingenium units, but timing chain rattle, CVVL system misfires, and turbo pipe issues are well-documented. The engine has an average lifespan of approximately 150,000–170,000 miles. With meticulous maintenance and premium fuel, many owners achieve high mileages — but these issues should be budgeted for.

mainEntity.name: What are the common problems with the Evoque P300?

acceptedAnswer.text: The main known issues are: (1) Timing chain rattle and stretch — the most critical and costly failure, (2) Misfire/rough running caused by CVVL system failure — subject to a JLR Service Action (SSM75724), and (3) Turbocharger pipe and oil pump issues — including weak phasers and failing turbo pipes.

mainEntity.name: Is it worth replacing an Evoque P300 engine, or should I scrap the car?

acceptedAnswer.text: Depends on vehicle value and condition. Against a well-kept £15,000–£25,000 Evoque P300, a £4,500–£6,500 recon is 18–43% of the car's value — often worth it given Evoque values have remained relatively strong. However, for higher-mileage examples or those with other issues, evaluate total repair costs before committing.

mainEntity.name: How do I find my Evoque P300 engine code?

acceptedAnswer.text: The P300 uses the PT204 (also known as AJ200P) — a 2.0-litre four-cylinder Ingenium petrol engine producing 300 PS and 400 Nm of torque. The engine code is stamped on the cylinder block. Tell us your registration and we'll confirm it for free.

mainEntity.name: Can I prevent timing chain failure on the P300?

acceptedAnswer.text: Prevention is not guaranteed, but you can reduce the risk: (1) Change oil every 10,000 miles or less with the correct JLR-spec oil — extended intervals accelerate chain wear, (2) Use premium unleaded fuel (95 RON minimum) — poor fuel quality can cause detonation and piston damage, (3) Never ignore cold-start rattle — book an inspection immediately if you hear it, and (4) On PHEV variants, be aware that the hybrid system can prevent the engine from reaching optimal operating temperature, accelerating wear.

eraMap.title: Land Rover Range Rover Evoque P300 — Which Engine Does Your Car Actually Have?

rows.eraNote: 2.0-litre four-cylinder Ingenium petrol with MHEV — 300 PS, 400 Nm. Timing chain, CVVL and turbo pipe issues documented

replacementCosts.h2: Land Rover Range Rover Evoque P300 Engine Replacement Costs
replacementCosts.labourEstimate: Fitting an Evoque P300 engine typically takes 12–16 hours at £65–£95/hr (independent). The transverse engine layout and MHEV system add complexity.

cta.label: → Compare Evoque P300 engine prices from UK specialists

problems.title: Timing Chain Rattle & Stretch
problems.symptoms: Rattling or dragging noise on cold start, rattle at 2,000–2,500 rpm, irregular engine running or rough idle, power loss or limp mode, engine management light with DTCs P0016/P0017
problems.typicalMileage: 60,000–100,000 miles — can occur earlier on PHEV variants

urgency.label: Immediate
urgency.text: if you hear rattle on cold start, book an inspection today

problems.recommendation: The PT204 timing chain system is a known weakness across Ingenium petrol engines. A Variable Camshaft Timing (VCT) unit internal seal below specification can cause rattle. In PHEV variants, the hybrid system prevents the engine from warming up properly, combined with long oil intervals, accelerating chain and tensioner wear. At the first sign of cold-start rattle, book an inspection immediately — a £1,500–£3,000 preventative chain replacement is dramatically cheaper than a £5,500–£8,500 engine replacement.

failureLink.label: Read the full timing chain failure guide → /failures/ingenium-petrol-timing-chain/ [NEW FAILURE PAGE NEEDED]

problems.title: Misfire / Rough Running (CVVL System)
problems.symptoms: Engine misfire on start-up or while running, rough idle, check engine light, DTCs P1315, P0316, P0300–P0304 stored
problems.replacementCost: N/A — repair only (does not progress to engine replacement if addressed)

urgency.label: Check
urgency.text: book inspection urgently if misfire or rough running develops

problems.recommendation: JLR Service Action SSM75724 confirms that Ingenium I4 2.0L petrol vehicles with misfire/rough running DTCs have the Continuous Variable Valve Lift (CVVL) system at fault. The diagnostic procedure involves checking for DTCs, running an engine speed sweep to identify misfire RPM, and replacing the CVVL unit if required. A £800–£1,500 repair is far cheaper than the engine damage that can follow if ignored.

failureLink.label: Read the full CVVL failure guide → /failures/cvvl-system-failure/ [NEW FAILURE PAGE NEEDED]

problems.title: Turbocharger Pipe & Oil Pump Issues
problems.symptoms: Loss of power, whistling noise from engine bay, check engine light, oil pressure warning
problems.repairCost: £500–£1,500 (turbo pipe replacement or oil pump repair)

urgency.label: Monitor
urgency.text: book inspection soon if symptoms appear

problems.recommendation: Common problems on the PT204 include failing turbo pipes, a malfunctioning oil pump, and weak phasers. Poor fuel quality can lead to detonation and piston destruction; exhaust manifold welds can burst and damage the turbine; Ti-VCT phase regulators are also a known weakness. If you notice loss of power or unusual noises, book an inspection promptly.

repairBuyOrReplace.h2: Evoque P300 — Repair, Buy, or Replace?

rows.whenItMakesSense: Preventative — catches the fault before catastrophic failure
Full engine replacement is the only reliable fix
JLR diagnostic procedure available — straightforward fix
Viable if caught before secondary damage occurs
Often requires full rebuild or replacement

buyingChecks.title: Buying This Evoque P300? Check These First
buyIf: * Full service history with 10,000-mile or less oil change intervals
* No cold-start rattle — this is a critical warning sign
* Evidence of premium fuel use (95 RON minimum)
* Remaining manufacturer warranty — these are complex vehicles
avoidIf: * Any cold-start rattle — this indicates timing chain wear
* Check engine light with misfire DTCs — CVVL system may be at fault
* Poor service history — the Ingenium petrol is unforgiving of neglect
* Whistling or siren-like noises from the engine bay — turbo pipe issues

repairBuyOrReplace.closingVerdict: The Evoque P300's PT204 Ingenium petrol engine offers strong performance — 300 PS and 0-60mph in 6.3–6.6 seconds. However, it shares the Ingenium family's well-documented weaknesses: timing chain rattle, CVVL system misfires, and turbo pipe failures. The petrol engine is generally more reliable than its diesel counterpart, but these issues are real and can be costly. Buy with full service history, evidence of premium fuel use, and no cold-start rattle. Without this? Approach with caution — the repair bills can be substantial.

cta.label: → Compare Evoque P300 engine prices before you buy

buyingChecklist.intro: Before you buy a used Land Rover Range Rover Evoque P300 (or before you commit to an engine replacement), check these:
items: * Cold Start — Listen for rattling or dragging noise from the timing chain area — this is critical
* Rough Running — Any misfire or rough idle? Check for check engine light
* Service History — Including the PT204 engine and confirmed 10,000-mile oil change intervals — non-negotiable
* Fuel Quality — Has the vehicle been run on premium unleaded (95 RON)?
* Turbo Noise — Listen for whistling or siren-like sounds under acceleration
* Engine Warning Light — Have codes been read? Look for P0016, P0017, P1315, P0300–P0304
* Oil Leaks — Check for leaks from the rear crankshaft oil seal
* Warranty Status — Manufacturer warranty is valuable on these complex vehicles
